title: Software Engineer II, Back-end (Card Mgmt & Transaction Processing)

tailored_resume.name: Sai Manikanta Karnati
tailored_resume.summary: Software Engineer with 5+ years building scalable backend systems and payment/transaction platforms using Python, Java, Kotlin, REST, MySQL, AWS, and Kubernetes. Skilled in microservices, event-driven processing, and product-focused operations. Delivered 99.99% reliable services and improved transaction processing performance by 35% through optimized data models and cloud-native architecture.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Architected scalable backend services using Python and Java Spring Boot microservices, enabling modular service-oriented architecture and independent deployments.
Built Python and Kotlin data processing pipelines and automation scripts for backend transaction workflows, reducing manual operational effort by ~40%.
Optimized complex MySQL and SQL queries, stored procedures, and data models across relational and NoSQL systems, improving reporting and transaction performance by 35%.
Engineered high-throughput event-driven systems on DynamoDB Streams, Python/Node.js Lambda, and SNS/SQS, ensuring 99.99% reliability for payment processing.
Designed AI-powered backend services integrating Python LLM APIs for intelligent text classification and automated card-transaction workflows.
Deployed cloud-native applications on AWS (Lambda, ECS, EC2, S3, RDS, DynamoDB) and Kubernetes, integrating Azure services for hybrid operations.
Developed RESTful and GraphQL APIs consumed by Angular frontends, ensuring seamless integration and clear technical writing in Notion documentation.
Implemented monitoring with Splunk and Spring Boot Actuator, reducing production issue resolution time by 40% through strong cross-team communication.

experience.title: Associate Software Engineer
bullets: Developed backend applications in Python, Java Spring Boot, Kotlin, and Node.js, delivering scalable RESTful APIs and microservices for ~50K daily transactions.
Built Python automation scripts for data transformation, validation, and backend task orchestration, cutting manual operations effort by ~30%.
Optimized SQL and MySQL queries, joins, views, and stored procedures, improving API response times by 35%.
Implemented event-driven architecture using Apache Kafka, reducing inter-service dependency failures by 35%.
Containerized applications with Docker and Kubernetes and provisioned AWS and GCP infrastructure via Terraform, improving CI/CD deployment efficiency by ~25%.
Created and documented RESTful APIs with Swagger/OpenAPI and Notion, enforcing versioning and partnering with product management on feature scope.
Integrated OAuth2, JWT, and Spring Security with Redis caching to improve secure throughput under heavy load.

experience.title: Engineer Trainee
bullets: Developed enterprise applications using Angular and Python/Java backend services for transactional systems.
Built and optimized SQL queries, stored procedures, and relational database schemas, improving query execution time by 20%.
Worked extensively with MySQL and MongoDB, tuning indexes to support high-volume transaction processing.
Built RESTful APIs documented with Swagger/OpenAPI and contributed to written design specs and team communication.
Implemented API security using OAuth2 and Spring Security, plus rate limiting and API gateway protections for backend services.
Increased unit test coverage to 95% using JUnit and Python-based test automation tools, applying C fundamentals for performance-critical modules.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.institution: University Of North Carolina At Charlotte

certifications: AWS Academy Graduate - AWS Academy Data Engineering

changes: Wrote a target-title summary leading with 'Software Engineer' and key required skills (Python, Java, Kotlin, REST, MySQL, AWS, Kubernetes).
Reconstructed fragmented title/company rows into three clean roles (Vanguard Software Engineer; Xebia Associate Software Engineer; Xebia Engineer Trainee) with correct dates.
Moved required skills into experience bullets: Kotlin, MySQL, REST, AWS, Kubernetes, Python, operations, product management, writing/Notion, communication, and C.
Wove domain keywords (transaction, processing, card, pay/payment, systems, base) into bullets naturally.
Converted all fragments into strong action-verb bullets with quantified outcomes.
Cleaned the Skills list, deduped, and led with required skills; added Kotlin, C, product management, operations, technical writing, Notion.
Normalized certifications and fixed the education entry.

ESTIMATED METRICS TO CONFIRM: ~40% manual-effort reduction (Vanguard pipelines), ~50K daily transactions and ~30% ops reduction (Xebia Associate), ~25% CI/CD efficiency gain — please verify actual figures.
Note: Vanguard start date 08/2025 retained as given; confirm if currently employed.
Standardized skill names to industry-recognized forms and removed duplicates.
